element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary combat mechanic throughout; scaling difficulty with enemy variety
Determines stat focus; Shamans uniquely gain magic access; class does not lock specialization
Earned through combat, missions, plot advancement; player allocates to specific stats
Mission variety increases; difficulty and pay scale with player negotiation stat; mission types: raiding, search & retrieval, extraction, courier, ghoul hunting, Matrix runs
Can hire for single run or lifetime; runner attitude affects price; player controls one character directly, others AI-controlled

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from game start (Shaman class only)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Offensive/defensive spellcasting; high-level spells damage caster; cyberware reduces magic effectiveness; mitigated by items or reducing spell power/success chance
Enhances combat (close-combat weapons, strength boost); reduces magic effectiveness if overused
Decker-focused; accessible to deckers and players with cyberdeck equipment
Running out of ammo forces melee fallback; resource management element

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Fallback early game / skill development mid-game
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Weak when untrained/physical weak; highly effective end-game with high skills + cyberware (spurs, hand razors)
Limited effectiveness relative to other combat methods
Multiple shop types: firearms/modifications, cyberware, spells/upgrades, cyberdecks, miscellaneous
Collectible NPCs providing information, services, illegal goods; late-game faction choices
Taxi default travel; visa requirements; helicopter bypass option for restricted areas
Initially restricted to single area; shortly gains access to almost all other areas

typical_relief_triggers: Hiring additional shadowrunners for difficult jobs
Dialogue with Mr. Johnsons and contacts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based pulses; real-time combat encounters vary in duration (relatively short) and difficulty; open-ended exploration between contracts allows player-controlled pacing; tension escalates through mission chain difficulty and runner availability/costs

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Mr. Johnson locations in backroom booths across different clubs/bars; contact availability varies by exploration; shop distribution across city areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player restricted to single area initially; early Mr. Johnsons offer basic missions; relatively short battles; emphasis on character build selection and initial Karma/equipment acquisition
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Area access expands significantly; mission variety increases (ghoul hunting, extractions, Matrix runs); runner recruitment enables tactical complexity; multiple mission difficulty tiers become available; magic/cyberware effectiveness becomes noticeable
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-difficulty shadowruns available; faction choices via contact system; melee combat highly viable with cyberware/skills; magic fully developed (for shamans); full party customization realized
notable_spikes: Transition from restricted single area to open exploration (access spike)
Matrix run introduction (new mission type, requires specialized build or equipment)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mr. Johnson backroom locations serve as mission hubs within clubs/bars; mission type, difficulty, and pay vary by Johnson; location distribution encourages spatial exploration between contracts
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any bar/club with Mr. Johnson booth

notable_level_patterns.description: Mission specifics vary by location type (Megacorp headquarters, LoneStar building, gang hangouts, ghoul dens); location determines environmental hazards and enemy composition
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Megacorp headquarters raid vs. gang hangout raid

notable_level_patterns.description: Battles are real-time and vary in difficulty; relatively short encounters encourage multiple runs; difficulty scaling tied to player negotiation stat and runner composition
Mission types branch: physical shadowruns (raids, extractions, ghoul hunting) vs. Matrix runs (cyberspace); deckers gain access to specialized mission type; duality adds replayability
